document.title: Relationship & Consent Extension Catalog — Heritage Gold → LAWIM_V2
document.description: Complete machine-readable catalog of all relationship and consent domain extensions from Heritage Gold → LAWIM_V2, including match consent tracking, proposal workflow, double consent model (C1 demandeur + C2 holder), relationship entity, participant roles, introduction tracking, shared data scope, consent lifecycle, revocation, expiration, idempotency, audit trail, and human handover workflows

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no consent tracking on matches; Match entity lacks demandeur/holder consent references, decision deadlines, and consent-linked state transitions
extensions.proposed_structure: ENRICH_EXISTING_ENTITY — Add consent-tracking fields to Match: demandeur_consent_id, holder_consent_id, double_consent_obtained_at, demandeur_decision (pending|interested|not_interested), holder_decision (pending|favorable|refused), decision_deadline. Add consent-aware states to Match state machine. Link Match lifecycle to double consent workflow.
required_permissions: Match: read consent status (all parties to the match)
Match: view consent history (assigned agent, admin)
required_states: Match.state ∈ {created, scored, proposed, demandeur_consent_pending, demandeur_consent_granted, holder_consent_pending, double_consent_obtained, relationship_established, active, expired, closed, archived}

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no proposal entity; match to consent transition lacks structured proposal with versioning, presentation, response tracking, or SLA
extensions.proposed_structure: CREATE_NEW_ENTITY — Implement Proposal entity with attributes: id, match_id, project_id, property_id, proposal_version, status (draft|sent|accepted|rejected|withdrawn|expired), presentation (JSON with score breakdown and property highlights), sent_at, responded_at, response (interested|not_interested|need_more_info), created_at, expires_at. Proposal flow: draft → sent → responded. SLA: 48h for demandeur response, 7d proposal validity.
required_states: Proposal.status ∈ {draft, sent, accepted, rejected, withdrawn, expired}
extensions.human_decision_question: Proposal validity SLA (default 48h for demandeur response) and auto-withdraw behavior

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no demandeur consent entity, no consent request lifecycle, no C1-specific SLA or channel tracking
extensions.proposed_structure: CREATE_NEW_ENTITY — Implement Consent entity with consent_type=demandeur_consent. Attributes: id, match_id, party_id, party_role=demandeur, consent_type, status (requested|granted|declined|expired|revoked), scope (JSON with purpose, proposal_id), channel, response_time, requested_at, responded_at, decline_reason?, expires_at (default 90d from grant). SLA: 48h from request to response. Reminder at 24h. Side effects: granted→initiate holder contact, declined→trigger rematching, expired→close consent.
required_permissions: Consent: view (match parties, assigned agent, admin)
extensions.human_decision_question: Demandeur consent SLA (default 48h) and reminder strategy

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no holder consent entity, no holder response handling (accept/refuse/delay/alternative/unavailable), no silence workflow, no per-property-type SLA
extensions.proposed_structure: CREATE_NEW_ENTITY — Implement Consent entity with consent_type=holder_consent. Attributes: id, match_id, party_id, party_role=holder, consent_type, status (requested|granted|declined|expired|revoked), scope (JSON with purpose, property_id), response (accept|refuse|delay|alternative|unavailable), alternative_property_id?, delay_duration?, reminder_count (0-3), last_reminder_at?, silence_workflow_exhausted, requested_at, responded_at, decline_reason?, expires_at (default 90d from grant). SLA: per property type (24h-30d). Silence workflow: 3 reminders + property marked 'to confirm' + rematch + LAWIM notify.
required_permissions: Consent: view (match parties, assigned agent, admin)
extensions.human_decision_question: Holder silence reminder intervals and property 'to confirm' behavior per property type

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has Conversation model linking user↔property but no formal Relationship entity with status machine, consent references, health score, anonymity tracking, or SLA enforcement
extensions.proposed_structure: CREATE_NEW_ENTITY — Implement Relationship entity with full state machine (10 states), double consent references, anonymity management, health scoring, inactivity SLA. Core attributes: id, match_id, proposal_id, demandeur_id, holder_id, property_id, demandeur_consent_id, holder_consent_id, status (proposed|consent_demandeur_pending|consent_demandeur_granted|consent_holder_pending|active|follow_up|expired|revoked|closed|archived), introduction_id?, shared_data_scope_id?, anonymity_lifted, anonymity_lifted_at?, interlocutor (proprietaire|agent|notaire|autre), agent_id?, visit_count, negotiation_status?, transaction_id?, health_score?, health_level?, last_activity_at, inactivity_alert_sent, follow_up_count, created_at, activated_at?, closed_at?, expires_at, version.
required_permissions: Relationship: update status (system via state machine)
Relationship: close (any party, assigned agent, admin)
required_states: Relationship.status ∈ {proposed, consent_demandeur_pending, consent_demandeur_granted, consent_holder_pending, active, follow_up, expired, revoked, closed, archived}
Terminal states: expired, revoked, closed, archived
extensions.human_decision_question: Relationship health score component weights and inactivity thresholds

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has User roles (buyer, seller, agent) but no relationship-specific role context, no agent opt-in workflow, no co-demandeur or guarantor support within a relationship
extensions.proposed_structure: ENRICH_EXISTING_ENTITY — Implement RelationshipParticipant junction table linking User to Relationship with role-specific context. Attributes: id, relationship_id, user_id, role (demandeur|holder|agent|co_demandeur|guarantor|notaire|professional|lawim_collaborator), joined_at, left_at?, status (active|inactive|removed), permission_level (full|limited|read_only). Add agent_optin workflow with 4-step process: detection → permission request → consent log → conditional share. Support extended roles: co-demandeur, guarantor, notaire, professional.
required_permissions: RelationshipParticipant: add (admin, system for agent routing)
RelationshipParticipant: remove (admin, own party for self-removal)
required_states: RelationshipParticipant.role ∈ {demandeur, holder, agent, co_demandeur, guarantor, notaire, professional, lawim_collaborator}
extensions.human_decision_question: Extended role support (co-demandeur, guarantor, notaire, professional) - which roles to implement in V1 vs defer

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no introduction tracking; no referral attribution, no commission eligibility tracking, no introducer role recording
extensions.proposed_structure: CREATE_NEW_ENTITY — Implement Introduction entity to track how parties were connected. Attributes: id, relationship_id, introduction_method (matching_engine|referral|agent_introduction|direct_search|manual), introducer_id? (User), introducer_role? (agent|user|lawim|system), referral_code?, referral_reward_claimed, commission_eligible, commission_amount?, commission_paid?, match_id, proposal_id, created_at. Link to referral reward system.
required_permissions: Introduction: read (relationship parties, assigned agent, admin)
extensions.human_decision_question: Referral reward amount and commission rate for agent introductions

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no per-relationship data sharing scope; contact coordinates are either fully visible or fully hidden with no granular control, no scope levels, no field-level sharing consent
extensions.proposed_structure: CREATE_NEW_ENTITY — Implement SharedDataScope entity for per-relationship data sharing. Attributes: id, relationship_id, scope_level (basic_contact|full_contact|professional_contact|temporary_access), fields_shared (String[]), granted_by_party (UUID), granted_at, expires_at?, is_active, revoked_at?, revoke_reason?. Enforce H0.5 privacy levels: PUBLIC fields always visible, PRIVATE require relationship consent, SENSITIVE require explicit data sharing consent, CONFIDENTIAL never shared via platform. Scope levels: basic_contact (phone), full_contact (phone+email+address), professional_contact (phone+email+agency, all relationships with same agent), temporary_access (phone, 7 days).
extensions.human_decision_question: Data sharing scope levels and default visibility per relationship phase

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no consent lifecycle state machine; no single consent record with requested→granted→revoked→expired states, no SLA enforcement per consent type, no renewal mechanism, no consent principles enforcement (explicit, informed, specific, revocable, recorded, time-bound, auditable)
extensions.proposed_structure: CONFIGURE_EXISTING — Implement Consent base entity with lifecycle state machine. States: requested → granted|declined → revoked|expired. Each consent has type (demandeur_consent|holder_consent|agent_optin|data_sharing), party_role, scope, channel, SLA, validity period, renewal mechanism. Enforce 7 consent principles: explicit (active opt-in only), informed (purpose+recipient+duration+revocation in request), specific (no blanket consent), revocable (revocation path on every consent), recorded (immutable audit), time-bound (expiration on every consent), auditable (full lifecycle trace). SLA: demandeur_consent 48h, holder_consent per property type, granted validity 90d default. Renewal request at T-7d.
required_permissions: Consent: view (affected parties, assigned agent, admin)
extensions.human_decision_question: Consent validity periods per type (default 90d) and renewal strategy

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no structured revocation workflow; no revocation state machine, no legal hold handling, no side-effect orchestration per consent type, no channel-specific revocation authentication
extensions.proposed_structure: CONFIGURE_EXISTING — Implement revocation sub-workflow on Consent. Revocation sources: user keyword (WhatsApp/Telegram with OTP), web/mobile click, support call. Immediate effect: status→revoked. Side effects within 1h: relationship termination (if C1/C2), data re-masking (if data_sharing), agent access revoked (if agent_optin). Notify counterparty within 1h. Legal hold: if active legal proceeding, enter pending_review state. Timeline: T+0 revoke, T+1h side effects, T+24h dependent processes terminated. Irreversible: new consent required for future ops.
required_permissions: Consent: legal hold (admin, legal team only)
extensions.human_decision_question: Legal hold override authority levels and revocation notification templates

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no centralized expiration handling; no pre-expiry warnings, no cascading expiration across related entities, no consent renewal mechanism, no post-expiry cleanup or archival workflow
extensions.proposed_structure: ENRICH_EXISTING_ENTITY — Implement centralized expiration engine with configurable per-entity SLA. Expiration sources: Match (expires_at), Proposal (7d), Consent C1 SLA (48h), Consent C1 validity (90d), Consent C2 SLA (per property type), Consent C2 validity (90d), Relationship inactivity (90d), SharedDataScope (per scope). Each source has: pre-expiry warning period, action on expiry, recovery path. Pre-expiry warnings: T-7d for validity, T-24h for SLA. Consent renewal: request at T-7d, auto-revoke on decline/no-response. Post-expiry: T+0 mark expired/execute side effects, T+90d auto-archive, T+180d anonymize.
required_fields: All entities with expiry: expires_at: DateTime
All entities with SLA: sla_deadline: DateTime?
extensions.human_decision_question: Consent renewal strategy: auto-renewal with notification vs explicit re-consent required

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no idempotency guarantees on consent operations; no idempotency keys, no unique constraints on business keys, no duplicate prevention on consent request/grant/revocation
extensions.proposed_structure: ADD_NEW_WORKFLOW — Implement idempotency layer across all consent and relationship operations. Idempotency keys per operation: create_consent_request (match_id+party_id+consent_type), grant_consent (consent_id+party_id), create_relationship (match_id), create_introduction (relationship_id), create_shared_data_scope (relationship_id+granting_party_id), revoke_consent (consent_id+revocation_request_id), renew_consent (consent_id+renewal_request_id). Enforcement: API gateway (Idempotency-Key header, 24h retention), service layer (unique constraint), database (composite unique indexes), event bus (dedup by event ID). Guard rules: duplicate consent request returns existing record, duplicate grant rejected by state machine, duplicate revocation is no-op, race condition resolved by first-response-wins.

extensions.source_concept: Audit trail for all consent changes
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no structured consent audit trail; no append-only consent_events table, no cryptographic hash chain, no relationship-scoped audit event catalog, no retention guarantee for consent records
extensions.proposed_structure: CREATE_NEW_ENTITY — Implement append-only ConsentAuditEvent table with immutable guarantees. Event types: consent.requested, consent.granted, consent.declined, consent.revoked, consent.expired, consent.renewed, consent.sla_reminder_sent, consent.silence_workflow_step, consent.auto_closed, consent.legal_hold_applied, consent.legal_hold_removed, consent.human_handover, consent.human_resolved. Additional relationship-scoped events: relationship.created, relationship.status_changed, relationship.party_added, relationship.party_removed, relationship.anonymity_lifted, relationship.data_shared, relationship.data_shared_revoked, relationship.visit_scheduled, relationship.negotiation_opened, relationship.closed, relationship.archived, relationship.reactivated. Each event record: audit_id (UUID), event_type, timestamp, consent_id?, relationship_id?, party_id?, actor_id?, actor_role, old_status?, new_status?, scope (JSON), metadata (JSON with channel, response_time, ip, user_agent, session_id), state_snapshot (JSON), signature (hash of previous event + payload hash). Guarantees: append-only, immutable (cryptographic hash chain), timestamped (NTP), traceable, never deleted.
required_permissions: ConsentAuditEvent: read (own party own consents, admin full)
extensions.human_decision_question: Audit event retention period (LAWIM heritage says NEVER deleted) and cryptographic hash chain implementation

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no human handover mechanism for failed automated consent; no escalation ticket system, no trigger detection for consent failures, no LAWIM collaborator dashboard for stuck consents
extensions.proposed_structure: CREATE_NEW_ENTITY — Implement HumanHandoverTicket entity for escalation from automated consent workflows. Triggers: silence exhaustion, technical failure (3 retry), ambiguous response, dispute flagged, legal hold, fraud suspicion, consent race condition, repeated expiry (3+). Each trigger has urgency (low|medium|high|urgent). Ticket attributes: id, consent_id?, relationship_id?, trigger (Enum), trigger_detail (String), urgency, status (open|assigned|in_progress|resolved|closed), assigned_to? (LAWIM collaborator), assigned_at?, resolution (consent_recorded|parties_contacted|system_retried|ticket_closed), resolution_note?, escalation_count, created_at, resolved_at. Human resolution actions: record consent manually, contact parties offline, force close, override SLA, mark property confirmed, trigger rematch, escalate to legal. SLA by urgency: urgent (15min assign, 2h resolve), high (1h/24h), medium (4h/48h), low (24h/72h). Escalation chain: L1 collaborator → L2 manager → L3 legal → L4 executive. Audit events for every handover step.
required_states: HumanHandoverTicket.trigger ∈ {silence_exhaustion, technical_failure, ambiguous_response, dispute_flagged, legal_hold, fraud_suspicion, race_condition, repeated_expiry}
extensions.human_decision_question: Human handover trigger conditions: which triggers warrant automatic escalation vs retry
